km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Sir John Forster於大約1525年出生在英國。他於大約1549年與Lady Jane Radclyff在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir John Forster于大约1525年出生在英国。他于大约1549年与Lady Jane Radclyff在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Forster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: shortened form of   Forrester  , an occupational name for someone who worked in a forest.
English: possibly a Norman French nickname or occupational name from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors or shears, cutler’. Compare Force and Forse .

German (mainly Förster): agent derivative of Middle High German forst ‘forest’, denoting someone who lived in or near a royal forest, or who worked in one (see Forst ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Foerster .

variants.name: Foster
meanings: English: variant of   Forster   ‘worker in a forest’.
English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English foster ‘foster parent’ (Old English fōstre, a derivative of fōstrian ‘to nourish or rear’). But other explanations are equally or more likely.
English: from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors’; see Forster 2.

variants.name: Forrest
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived in or near a royal forest, or a metonymic occupational name for a keeper or worker in one. Middle English forest was not, as today, a near-synonym of wood, but referred specifically to a large area of woodland reserved by law for the purposes of hunting by the king and his nobles. The same applied to the European cognates, both ancient Germanic and Romance. The English word is from Middle English forest ‘forest’, Old French forest, Late Latin forestis (silva). This is generally taken to be a derivative of foris ‘outside’; the reference was probably to woods lying outside a settlement. On the other hand, Middle High German for(e)st has been held to be a derivative of Old High German foraha ‘fir’ (see Forster ), with the addition of a collective suffix.
Irish: this name is also frequently attested in Ireland, where it may be a variant of   Forrestal  .

variants.name: Forrester
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English forester ‘officer in charge of a forest or lord's hunting ground’ (Old French forestier, Anglo-Norman forester). See Forrest .

variants.name: Fuster
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Armando, Blanca, Ricardo, Carlos, Pedro, Vicente, Alfredo, Ana, Angelina, Barbaro.
Catalan: occupational name from fuster ‘carpenter’.
German and Jewish: variant of   Forster   3 and 4.

variants.name: Foerst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Dieter, Klaus.
German (Först): shortened form of Förster or Förstner (see Forster 3). Compare Forst .

variants.name: Forsmann
meanings: North German: variant of Forstmann, itself a variant of Förster (see Forster ).

variants.name: Forstner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Gerhard, Erna, Heinz, Ignaz, Ilse, Monika.
German (also Förstner): variant of Forster .
Slovenian (also Forštner): topographic or occupational name for someone who lived or worked in a forest, of German origin (see above and Forster ).

variants.name: Ferster
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt.
German: variant of Förster (see Forster 3).

variants.name: Foerster
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerda, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Claus, Erwin, Alois, Bernd, Christoph, Detlef, Erna, Gerd.
German (Förster): see Forster .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Sir John Forster was born about 1525, in England. He married Lady Jane Radclyff about 1549, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir John Forster est né environ 1525, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Lady Jane Radclyff environ 1549, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Sir John Forster wurde um 1525 in England geboren. Er hat um 1549 in England Lady Jane Radclyff geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Sir John Forster è nato il circa 1525 nel England. Lui sposato Lady Jane Radclyff circa 1549, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Forsterは約1525年にイギリスで生まれた。約1549年にイギリスでLady Jane Radclyffと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Forster(은)는 대략 1525년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 대략 1549년 영국에서 Lady Jane Radclyff(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir John Forster nasceu aproximadamente 1525, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Lady Jane Radclyff aproximadamente 1549, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir John Forster родился около 1525 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Jane Radclyff около 1549 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Sir John Forster nació aproximadamente 1525, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Lady Jane Radclyff aproximadamente 1549, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
